name: Southeast Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured regional bank serving Middle and East Tennessee with checking, savings, mortgages, business lending, and personal loan products.
description_long:
SouthEast Bank is a regional financial institution operating in Middle and East Tennessee with FDIC insurance backing all deposits. The bank emphasizes personal service and local decision-making, positioning itself as a trusted financial partner for both individuals and businesses in its service area. The bank has recently published messaging about its financial strength and stability in response to banking industry concerns.

SouthEast Bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail and commercial banking products. For consumers, this includes multiple checking account options (Rewards Checking earning $0.10 cashback per debit card transaction of $10+, Bonus Rate Checking up to 3.30% APY, Premium Interest Checking with tiered rates), savings accounts, Certificates of Deposit, student loans (private and parent loans with refinancing), personal loans, home equity lines of credit and loans, home construction loans, credit cards, and mortgage services including refinancing. For businesses, they provide commercial checking and savings accounts, business credit cards, commercial lending, construction and development financing, real estate loans, equipment financing, lines of credit, treasury management, and merchant services.

SouthEast Bank distinguishes itself through emphasis on personalized, local service and relationship-based banking. Their Bonus Rate Checking account offers competitive rates (up to 3.30% APY on balances up to $20,000) compared to national averages, marketed as earning "47x the national average." The bank provides both in-branch and online banking options, appointment scheduling with relationship managers, and branch locations throughout their Tennessee service area. They explicitly reference their financial strength and security in recent messaging to reassure customers.

As a regional bank, SouthEast Bank is best suited for customers in Middle and East Tennessee seeking personalized service and relationship banking rather than digital-first alternatives. The bank's strength in both consumer and commercial lending provides comprehensive options for families and business owners in their geographic footprint. Primary limitations include geographic restriction to Tennessee, and typical regional bank constraints compared to large national banks regarding product breadth and digital innovation.
services: Personal Checking Accounts (Rewards Checking, Bonus Rate Checking, Premium Interest Checking)
Personal Savings Accounts and Certificates of Deposit
Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOC) and Home Equity Loans
Personal Loans and Home Construction Loans
Private Student Loans and Parent Loans with student loan refinancing
Commercial lending including business loans, construction/development financing, real estate loans
Business credit cards and lines of credit
pros: Competitive Bonus Rate Checking offering up to 3.30% APY on balances up to $20,000 (marketed as 47x national average)
Rewards Checking account with $0.10 cashback on every debit card purchase of $10 or more
FDIC-insured deposits with emphasis on financial strength and stability
Comprehensive lending products including mortgages, home equity loans, commercial loans, and equipment financing
Relationship-based service model with local decision-making and ability to schedule appointments with managers
Both personal and business banking offerings covering checking, savings, CDs, and various loan products
Online and in-branch banking options with branch network throughout Middle and East Tennessee
cons: Geographic limitation to Middle and East Tennessee only, not available nationally
Bonus Rate Checking requires meeting unspecified requirements to earn advertised 3.30% APY
Limited details on APR rates for credit cards, personal loans, and other lending products on website
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or advisory services beyond insurance offerings
Regional bank with likely smaller digital banking platform compared to national competitors
best_for: Tennessee residents in Middle and East regions seeking personalized banking relationships
Small business owners and entrepreneurs needing local commercial lending with relationship managers
Consumers prioritizing high-yield checking accounts and competitive savings rates
Homebuyers and homeowners in Tennessee seeking mortgage and home equity lending products
diagnosis: SouthEast Bank is best for Tennessee residents and businesses in Middle and East regions seeking relationship-based banking with competitive rates on checking and savings products, combined with comprehensive lending options including mortgages and commercial financing. The main caveat is geographic restriction to Tennessee—customers outside this region will not have access to branches or local relationship services, and the bank does not appear to offer nationwide online banking for out-of-state customers.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
